summary: Expanded decompilation and documentation for the VS Records mndiagram menu modules, especially by starting substantial matching work on mndiagram3. The PR added a documented Diagram3 user-data struct, new mndiagram3 prototypes/static externs, and several matched or improved functions for page 3 ranking display creation, animation, scroll arrows, and cleanup. It also refined mndiagram and mndiagram2 matching through type/prototype corrections, helper selection between name/fighter lookup paths, pointer-base arithmetic against asset tables, explicit proc flag assignment, stack padding, and small mnvibration formatting/matching tweaks. The decomp.dev report recorded 6 new matches and +1144 matched bytes overall, with mndiagram3 functions accounting for most new matches.
what_changed: Added forward declaration typedef struct Diagram3 Diagram3 in src/melee/mn/forward.h.

Added a documented struct Diagram3 in src/melee/mn/types.h for VS Records page 3, size 0x78, with saved menu/selection state, scroll offset, animation state, name-mode flag, 10 JObj pointers, 10 row label HSD_Text pointers, title/value text pointers, 5 row icon text pointers, and popup_gobj.
Expanded src/melee/mn/mndiagram3.c from a near-stub into about 570 lines of decompiled code for VS Records page 3 creation and display logic.
Added mndiagram3 routines including mnDiagram3_80245BA4, mnDiagram3_80246D40, fn_80246E04, fn_80246E64, mnDiagram3_80246F2C, mnDiagram3_80247008, and mnDiagram3_8024714C.

Implemented page 3 ranking display construction using mnDiagram2 ranking/stat helpers, HSD text creation, fighter icon JObjs, stat formatting for time/distance/percentage/decimal values, overflow handling, and row label/icon rendering.
Implemented mndiagram3 user-data initialization, GObj/JObj creation, archive loading, animation setup, popup placement, scroll arrow visibility updates, and transition/cleanup behavior.
Added src/melee/mn/mndiagram3.static.h with externs for mndiagram3 asset tables, archive data, globals, assert strings, colors, and float constants.
Updated src/melee/mn/mndiagram3.h to include needed baselib and mn types and expose address-commented prototypes for the newly decompiled functions.

Improved mndiagram.c matching by changing mnDiagram_803EE728 from Vec3 to Point3d, using Menu_GetAllInputs for popup/input procs, storing the HSD_GObjProc returned by HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54, assigning proc->flags_3, adding dont_inline around mnDiagram_CountUnlockedFighters, and reshaping local declarations/order/pointer arithmetic in popup animation and layout code.

Improved mndiagram2.c matching by splitting header entity lookup into mnDiagram_GetNameByIndex_s and mnDiagram_GetFighterByIndex_s, adjusting field writes to mn_804A04F0.buttons, adding PAD_STACK usage, changing several temporaries from u8 to int/u32, correcting text_color globals, using byte-base table arithmetic for AnimLoopSettings, and changing mnDiagram2_InitUserData to accept an unused int argument.

Refactored mnDiagram2_Create to preserve matching-sensitive control flow: selected entity/mode setup, scroll loop as do-while, threshold handling for name vs fighter mode, and UpdateHeader call with selected index.
Improved ranking functions in mndiagram2, including mnDiagram2_GetRankedFighter changes around entry filling, integer casts, PAD_STACK, and selection-sort/insertion-shift logic comments.
Adjusted static header declarations for mndiagram and mndiagram2 to use explicit char array sizes for small data strings and added/renamed externs as needed.
Made minor mnvibration.c formatting/matching changes around explicit pointer arithmetic for HSD_PadCopyStatus and GObj object setup casts.
smart_moves: Introduced Diagram3 as a real typed user-data struct before expanding mndiagram3 logic, making offsets and ownership of JObjs/text objects explicit instead of leaving the module as raw pointer arithmetic.

Reused existing mndiagram2 stat helpers for mndiagram3 ranking display rather than duplicating stat interpretation: mnDiagram2_GetRankedName, mnDiagram2_GetRankedFighter, mnDiagram2_GetStatValue, mnDiagram2_IsTimeStat, mnDiagram2_IsDistanceStat, mnDiagram2_IsPercentageStat, mnDiagram2_IsIconOnlyStat, and aggregated fighter rank helpers.

Kept address-based temporary names for still-unknown functions and data while adding typed prototypes and comments, preserving searchability without inventing semantic names not supported by evidence.

Used byte-base casts such as char* base or u8* base plus explicit offsets for mixed asset/data tables; this helped represent tables containing Point3d, AnimLoopSettings, text IDs, and constants without overclaiming structure layouts.
Assigned proc->flags_3 after HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54 in both mndiagram and mndiagram3 transition paths, matching the proc scheduling pattern used by the menu system.
Used explicit stack padding pragmas/macros and dont_inline control where needed for codegen, including PAD_STACK additions and #pragma dont_inline on mnDiagram_CountUnlockedFighters.
Separated name-mode and fighter-mode entity lookup paths in mndiagram2_UpdateHeader and creation logic, which documents behavior while improving matching.
decomp_lessons: For menu pages backed by GObj user data, defining the user-data struct with verified offsets can unlock much cleaner matching in creation, animation, and display functions.

When a data blob is not yet fully modeled, byte-base pointer arithmetic can be a practical matching tactic for accessing embedded vectors, animation loop settings, text IDs, and constants at exact offsets.

Small type changes matter for PowerPC codegen: Vec3 versus Point3d, u8 versus int/u32, s32 versus int, u16 versus int, and explicit casts at call sites can shift register use and comparisons enough to match.
HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54 return values may need to be captured so proc fields such as flags_3 can be set; omitting this can leave otherwise correct control-flow code unmatched.
Input helper selection affects generated code. This PR moved some mndiagram input paths from mn_80229624(4) to Menu_GetAllInputs with u64 input and explicit u32 masks.

For ranking/stat UI functions, format-specific caps and conversions should be kept close to the stat type checks: time max 0x5C8D7F, distance conversion/overflow handling, percentage max 0x98967F, and general decimal formatting are all visible matching details here.

Creation loops often need original loop shape, not just equivalent behavior. The mndiagram2 and mndiagram3 code uses do-while loops, explicit scroll wraparound, and name/fighter threshold constants 0x18 and 0x15.
Static headers should declare exact array sizes for small rodata/sdata strings when known; this PR replaced unsized SDATA char declarations with explicit char arrays in some mndiagram headers.
naming_conventions: New page 3 symbols follow the existing module-address convention: mnDiagram3_80245BA4, mnDiagram3_80246D40, mnDiagram3_80246F2C, mnDiagram3_80247008, mnDiagram3_8024714C.
Local static/global data names retain address-based naming such as mnDiagram3_803EEC10, mnDiagram3_803EEC1C, mnDiagram3_803EEC28, mnDiagram3_803EEC4C, mnDiagram3_804D6C20, and mnDiagram3_804DC00C.
Unknown local functions remain fn_address style, including fn_80246E04, fn_80246E64, fn_80246F0C, and static-header prototype fn_802461BC.
Typed user-data structs use page-oriented names Diagram, Diagram2, and newly Diagram3, with a matching forward declaration in mn/forward.h.
Static-private declarations are grouped in module .static.h files, with mndiagram3.static.h exporting mndiagram2.static.h and mndiagram3.h via IWYU pragmas.

Documented struct fields use behavior-derived names where evidence is clear: saved_menu, saved_selection, scroll_offset, anim_state, is_name_mode, jobjs, row_labels, title_text, value_text, row_icons, popup_gobj.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used #pragma dont_inline around mnDiagram_CountUnlockedFighters to preserve expected call/inlining behavior.
Added PAD_STACK in functions where stack layout mattered, including mnDiagram_PopupAnimProc, mnDiagram2_HandleInput, mnDiagram2_GetRankedFighter, and several mndiagram3 functions.
Used explicit base pointer arithmetic, for example casting &mnDiagram2_803EEAD0 or &mnDiagram3_803EEC10 to u8*/char* before adding offsets for AnimLoopSettings, vectors, text IDs, and constants.
Changed some array accesses to casts through byte pointers, such as Point3d* casts against mnDiagram_803EE728 base offsets 0x0, 0xC, and 0x18.
Manipulated mn_804A04F0.buttons through s32 pointer casts in mnDiagram2_HandleInput, suggesting codegen depended on exact 32-bit writes into a wider button field.
Captured HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54 return values and assigned proc->flags_3 = HSD_GObj_804D783C in popup/transition paths.
Used explicit casts to u8 at entity lookup boundaries while keeping temporaries as int/u32 when that better matched comparisons and register allocation.
Preserved assert calls with exact module string globals and line numbers in mndiagram3 object lookup/loading paths.
Used direct JObj translate field accesses and dirty-matrix updates, including HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty or ftCo_800C6AFC when flags did not include 0x02000000.
For mnvibration, preserved a mulli pattern by expressing HSD_PadCopyStatus indexing as explicit byte-pointer arithmetic with stride 0x44 and offset 0x41.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The PR body described the work as still WIP and as 'some more matches + documentation in mndiagram & mndiagram2' plus starting mndiagram3 matching.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code increasing to 53.93%, +0.03%, +1144 bytes.
The bot reported 6 new matches: mndiagram3_80247008, mndiagram3_80246F2C, fn_80246E64, fn_80246E04, mnDiagram_PopupInputProc, and mnDiagram2_PopulateStatRows.

The bot reported 18 improvements in unmatched items, notably mndiagram3_80245BA4 to 91.47%, mndiagram3_8024714C to 90.20%, mndiagram3_80246D40 to 98.57%, mnDiagram2_Create to 93.77%, and several mndiagram/mndiagram2 functions.
The bot also reported 2 regressions in unmatched items: mnDiagram_80242C0C decreased and mndiagram2 .sdata2 decreased, so future work should recheck those areas.
follow_up_queries: Inspect later PRs touching mnDiagram_80242C0C to see whether the regression reported by decomp.dev was recovered.
Search for further semantic renames of mnDiagram3_80245BA4, mnDiagram3_80246D40, mnDiagram3_80247008, and mnDiagram3_8024714C after this initial mndiagram3 matching work.
Check subsequent changes to Diagram3 in src/melee/mn/types.h for field validation, padding corrections, or renamed members.
Look for future work modeling mnDiagram3_803EEC10 and related data blobs as typed structs rather than byte-offset accesses.
Compare later mndiagram2 ranking-function PRs for completion of mnDiagram2_GetRankedFighter and mnDiagram2_GetRankedName matching.
Search for other menu modules that use HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54 followed by proc->flags_3 assignment as a reusable pattern.
